message:
fix(hash,schema): align content_hash algorithm with Go bd + v7 migration recomputes stored hashes

Beads currently ships two content-hash algorithms: Go bd's canonical
writer and Rust beads_rust's port. They were never byte-for-byte
identical — Rust prefixed priority with "P" ("P2" vs "2"), used
"true"/"false" for booleans, newly added Go fields weren't represented
at all, and the null-escaping fallback rewrote embedded NULs before
hashing. So the same Issue, round-tripped through the two tools, got
different hashes. Cross-tool deduplication (import-from-bd, export-to-bd,
shared JSONL mirrors) couldn't trust content_hash.

Reconcile by matching Go bd exactly. Storage gets a v7 migration to
recompute the stored hashes so existing rows line up with the new
algorithm immediately.

src/util/hash.rs — algorithm changes:
- Priority is written as `"2"` not `"P2"` (drop the prefix).
- Boolean flags use Go's "empty-or-label" encoding: `pinned` is
  either the literal `"pinned"` or `""`, same for `"template"`.
  Replace `field_bool(value: bool)` with
  `field_flag(value: bool, label: &str)`.
- Append empty/default placeholders for the Go bd fields Rust
  doesn't yet model:
    - `quality_score` → "" (nil)
    - `crystallizes` flag → "" (false)
    - `await_type` → ""
    - (additional fields follow the same pattern)
  Hashing their Go zero values keeps Rust byte-for-byte compatible
  for every field Go writes, even when Rust hasn't adopted the
  column yet.
- `HashFieldWriter::field` no longer tries to escape embedded NULs.
  Go bd writes raw bytes-then-separator, trusting that its own data
  layer doesn't emit NULs. The Rust escaping was well-intentioned
  but broke parity whenever a field legitimately carried a unicode
  NUL (rare, but the mismatch was silent and permanent).
- Top-of-file doc updated to call out the empty-placeholder
  invariant.

src/model/mod.rs:
- Tighten the `Issue::content_hash` doc comment to name the Go bd
  canonical order and enumerate the exclusion set (IDs, timestamps,
  relations, tombstone metadata). The earlier enumeration was
  outdated — it omitted `quality_score`, `crystallizes`,
  `await_type`, and the other placeholder fields the new algorithm
  accounts for.

src/storage/schema.rs — v7 migration:
- Bump `CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION` from 6 to 7.
- New `rebuild_content_hashes_for_go_parity` runs on every DB whose
  user_version is below 7 and has an `issues` table. It:
    1. Queries every row, reconstructing the typed fields via
       `Status`/`Priority`/`IssueType` parsers.
    2. Recomputes the hash through `content_hash_from_parts` — the
       same function production writes use, so the migration and
       the live writer can't drift.
    3. Writes the new hash back via `UPDATE issues SET
       content_hash = ? WHERE id = ?`.
    4. Returns the count of rows touched for tracing.
- The migration is logged at info as "Migrating database to schema
  version 7 (Go bd content hashes)" so operators see when it kicks
  in. JSONL flush metadata will naturally follow because the stored
  hash and the algorithmic recompute now agree.

Effect: import/export between bd and beads_rust produces identical
content_hash values for the same logical issue, and the next `br
sync --flush-only` after this migration writes a JSONL whose hashes
match what bd would produce.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
